applied_optimizations.name: Hybrid approach with Triton fused Swish+bias kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Used hybrid approach: PyTorch Linear (rocBLAS) for GEMM, Triton @triton.jit kernel for fused Swish+bias, PyTorch GroupNorm. The Triton kernel fuses sigmoid*mul+bias into single memory pass, reducing kernel launches and memory bandwidth. BLOCK_SIZE=256 for AMD wavefront efficiency.

attempted_but_failed.name: Naive Triton GEMM
attempted_but_failed.reason: Significantly slower than rocBLAS/hipBLASLt vendor-tuned kernels

attempted_but_failed.name: Triton GroupNorm (3-pass)
attempted_but_failed.reason: 2x slower than PyTorch optimized GroupNorm, plus numerical precision issues

attempted_but_failed.name: Swish(x + linear_bias) + bias kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Incorrect - was adding linear_bias twice since Linear already includes bias

top_bottlenecks: GEMM (PyTorch Linear) - dominant compute, well-optimized by rocBLAS
Elementwise ops - fused into single Triton kernel

notes: Key insight: Don't fight vendor-tuned kernels (GEMM, GroupNorm) - use them
Triton is effective for fusing elementwise ops to reduce memory passes
Hybrid approach: PyTorch for heavy compute, Triton for elementwise fusion
Important: Linear layer already includes bias, don't add it again in fusion

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Trial 4 produced no output — this is the 3rd consecutive silent failure. The agent needs an explicit, complete code template to copy into generated_kernel.py. Providing the full fused swish+bias+Group

attempted_but_failed.reason: 5th consecutive trial with no output. The agent keeps getting stuck. Providing a complete, copy-paste-ready implementation that fuses swish+bias+GroupNorm into a single Triton kernel while keeping PyT
